title: Markdown component with syntax highlighting and custom renderers
purpose: Renders markdown content with custom styling and syntax highlighting for code blocks, supporting both light and dark themes.
module_context: Exports a Markdown React component that accepts markdown content and various rendering options, including code highlighting and HTML allowance.

steps.content: Determine if the current theme is dark mode based on system preference.
Define custom renderer components for various markdown elements including headers, paragraphs, lists, tables, code blocks, and links.
Configure the ReactMarkdown component with custom renderers and appropriate remark plugins based on props.
Render the markdown content within a styled container with appropriate accessibility attributes.
Check if the paragraph children contain block-level elements such as code blocks or divs.
IF block-level elements are detected, render the paragraph as a div to avoid invalid nesting.
ELSE render the paragraph as a standard p element with appropriate styling.

methods.name: code
methods.normalized_params: ({ node, inline, className, children, ...props }: any): React.ReactElement

steps.content: Extract the language from the code block's class name if present.
Determine if the code block should be rendered as a block element based on inline property and language class.
IF the code block is a block and highlighting is enabled, render with syntax highlighting.
ELSE render inline code with appropriate styling.
ELSE render as a preformatted block with appropriate styling.
